articles.title: Market Shifts: Eurozone Inflation Under Control as Bund Yields Dive
articles.description: German 10-year Bund yields reached a three-month low due to falling oil prices and reassurance from ECB President Christine Lagarde about limited inflation. This trend suggests less aggressive policy action by the ECB. The decline reflects a shift in market sentiment, partly due to geopolitical developments involving the U.S. and Iran.

articles.title: Twin petrol bomb incidents rock Bathinda during CM Mann’s stay; 5 apprehended in one case
articles.description: 4 accused have been arrested in the Amarpura Basti attack, while one juvenile has been apprehended; no breakthrough in the homoeopathy clinic blast.

articles.title: Trump accuses big oil companies of ‘gouging’ consumers as gasoline prices fail to match falling crude costs, orders DOJ
articles.description: The directive comes as fuel prices remain elevated despite a sharp decline in global oil prices following an interim peace agreement between the United States and Iran.

articles.title: 26-Year-Old Man Set On Fire After Refusing Petrol In Gwalior, Suffers 60% Burns
articles.description: A 26-year-old man, Akash Sengar, was allegedly set on fire after refusing to give petrol during an argument in Thatiapur area on Tuesday night. His father suffered burn injuries while rescuing him. The suspect and an associate fled. Both are undergoing treatment at JA Group of Hospitals. Police are investigating and checking CCTV footage probe is continuing.

articles.title: U.S. shares edge higher in early trading
articles.description: Stocks are edging higher on Wall Street as falling bond yields and lower oil prices help ease pressure on the market.

articles.title: Fuel prices June 25 - July 1: Diesel, unsubsidised RON97, RON95 prices down
articles.description: KUALA LUMPUR: The retail price of diesel in Peninsular Malaysia will decrease by 30 sen to RM4.07 per litre from June 25 to 30, while unsubsidised RON97 and RON95 petrol prices will each drop by 25 sen to RM4.10 and RM3.47 per litre, respectively.

articles.title: Why Trump suddenly sounds a lot like Biden on gas prices
articles.description: President Donald Trump says “Big Oil” companies are keeping gas prices artificially high, echoing a complaint former President Joe Biden made during the last gas price spike. But, unlike Biden, Trump says he wants the Justice Department to investigate.

articles.title: Why Trump suddenly sounds a lot like Biden on gas prices
